'''Riley Dean''' (born 10 August 2001) is an {{rlnt|Ireland}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{Rlp|so}} or {{Rlp|sh}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from Warrington at the [[York City Knights]], [[Dewsbury Rams]], [[Newcastle Thunder]], and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and at the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. In 2024 he joins the [[Mackay  Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]]. They are the feeder club for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[NRL]].

==Club career==
===Warrington Wolves===
In 2019 he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves v St Helens">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP2921808|title=Warrington Wolves v St Helens|publisher=bbc.co.uk|date=8 August 2019|accessdate=8 August 2019}}</ref>

==== York City Knights (loan) ====
On 25 February 2021 it was reported that he had signed on loan for the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Feb 2021|accessdate=25 Feb 2021|title=Warrington's Riley Dean joins York on loan as Morgan Smith suffers season-ending injury|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warringtons-riley-dean-joins-york-on-loan-as-morgan-smith-suffers-season-ending-injury/}}</ref>

==== Castleford Tigers (loan) ====
On 13 June 2023, the [[Castleford Tigers]] announced the signing of Dean on a season-long loan deal, with Warrington holding a two-week recall option.<ref>{{Cite web |date=13 June 2023 |title=Riley Dean: Castleford Tigers sign Warrington Wolves half-back on loan |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65897203 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=13 June 2023 |title=Dean joins Cas on loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8636 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref> Head coach [[Andy Last]] believed his arrival would add balance to the team amidst several injury absences.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=20 June 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers boss hoping that new recruit Riley Dean will add balance to his team |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-hoping-that-new-recruit-riley-dean-will-add-balance-to-his-team-4188657 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=30 June 2023 |title=Riley Dean: Castleford Tigers loanee set for debut as coach provides latest on half-back's future |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/riley-dean-castleford-tigers-debut-loan-from-warrington-wolves |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Dean made his Castleford debut against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] on 30 June, kicking a 40-20.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Staniforth |first=Mark |date=30 June 2023 |title=Riley Dean unable to help Castleford Tigers thwart champions St Helens |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/riley-dean-unable-to-help-castleford-tigers-thwart-champions-st-helens-4204006 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=30 June 2023 |title=Super League: St Helens 22-0 Castleford Tigers - Saints bounce back to earn comfortable win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66032423 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 1 August, Warrington recalled Dean from the loan spell.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=1 August 2023 |title=Warrington Wolves recall playmaker as international forward joins Super League rivals |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/warrington-wolves-recall-playmaker-as-international-forward-joins-super-league-rivals |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Turner |first=Matt |date=1 August 2023 |title=Warrington Wolves recall Riley Dean from Castleford loan |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/23693088.warrington-wolves-recall-riley-dean-castleford-loan/ |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Warrington Guardian}}</ref> In total, Dean made 4 appearances and kicked 2 goals for the Tigers.<ref name="RLRKC" /><ref>{{Cite web |date=1 August 2023 |title=Dean recalled from loan |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8704 |access-date=3 August 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

===Mackay Cutters===
On 2 November 2023, it was announced Dean would leave Warrington to take up an opportunity in Australia after making only ten appearances for the club. On 23 November 2023 it was announced he had signed for the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-warrington-wolves-starlet-riley-dean-sees-australia-move-confirmed-after-departing-super-league-club |title=Former Warrington Wolves starlet Riley Dean sees Australia move confirmed after departing Super League club |website=Love Rugby League |date=23 November 2023 |access-date=18 January 2024}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 19 Jul 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=19 Jul 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Former Warrington Wolves and Castleford Tigers halfback Riley Dean makes UK move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-warrington-wolves-and-castleford-tigers-halfback-riley-dean-makes-uk-move/}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 20 May 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] in a deal until the end of the 2026 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 May 2026|author=Aaron Bower|title=Bradford Bulls land surprise deal for Championship star in immediate transfer|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-sign-riley-dean}}</ref>

==International==
On 16 April 2020 it was announced that Dean had been called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the upcoming [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] qualifiers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://warringtonwolves.com/players/23402/|title=Riley Dean - Player Profile|website=www.warringtonwolves.com|accessdate=17 Dec 2020}}</ref> Captained the first ever Ireland RL U16 team.

He eventually made his debut for {{rlnt|Ireland}} in the win over {{rlnt|Scotland}} in October 2024, scoring 6/7 goals.
